seoTitle: Fusemi (Senwei) Solutions | Motor Drives, EV, Solar Applications
seoDescription: Explore Fusemi power semiconductor solutions for industrial motor drives, electric vehicles, and renewable energy applications.

faqs.question: What industries does Fusemi serve?
faqs.answer: Fusemi serves: Industrial Automation (motor drives, servo), Electric Vehicles (traction inverters, OBC), Renewable Energy (solar, wind, storage), Power Supplies (server, UPS), Welding Equipment, and Medical Equipment.
faqs.decisionGuide: Identify your industry and explore our industry-specific solutions.

faqs.question: When should I use SiC vs IGBT?
faqs.answer: Use SiC for: >600V applications, high-frequency operation (>20kHz), high-efficiency requirements, high-temperature environments. Use IGBT for: <600V applications, cost-sensitive designs, standard industrial frequencies (5-10kHz).
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SiC for high-efficiency/high-frequency. Use IGBT for cost-sensitive/standard frequency.

solutions.name: Industrial Motor Drive Solution
solutions.description: Complete power semiconductor solution for industrial motor drives from 0.5kW to 500kW. Features Fusemi IGBT modules and MOSFETs optimized for motor control applications with robust protection and high reliability.
features: Integrated power modules (IPM) for simplified design
Low switching losses for high efficiency

keyComponents.description: Integrated power module for compact drives

coreAdvantages.title: Complete Product Range
coreAdvantages.description: From small IPMs to large IGBT modules covering all power levels.

coreAdvantages.title: High Reliability
coreAdvantages.description: Industrial-grade components with comprehensive protection features.

customerCases.challenge: Needed reliable IGBT modules for 15kW motor drives with high efficiency and robust protection.
customerCases.solution: Fusemi SWA50-1200T IGBT modules with complete technical support and thermal design assistance.
customerCases.results: Achieved >97% efficiency with excellent reliability. Production volume exceeded 10,000 units with <100ppm field failure rate.

faeInsights.insight: Motor drive applications require careful thermal management and protection design. Always verify short-circuit withstand capability and thermal performance under worst-case conditions.
faeInsights.logic: Motor drive design follows these steps: First, determine power level and voltage class. Second, select appropriate IGBT or IPM based on current requirements. Third, design thermal management system. Fourth, implement protection circuits. Fifth, optimize switching frequency for efficiency vs EMI.
keyTakeaways: Verify thermal design under worst-case conditions
Consider switching frequency for efficiency trade-offs
bestPractices: Perform thermal simulation under worst-case load

faqs.question: What protection is needed for motor drives?
faqs.answer: Motor drives need: Short-circuit protection for IGBTs, Over-current protection for output stages, Over-voltage protection for DC link, Under-voltage protection, Thermal protection, and Motor over-temperature protection. All protections should be coordinated to prevent damage while allowing normal operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Implement all standard protections for reliable operation.

solutions.name: EV Powertrain Solution
solutions.description: High-efficiency power semiconductor solution for electric vehicle traction inverters and onboard chargers. Features SiC MOSFETs and automotive-grade IGBT modules for maximum efficiency and reliability.

coreAdvantages.title: Highest Efficiency
coreAdvantages.description: SiC devices enable >98% efficiency reducing cooling requirements and extending range.

coreAdvantages.title: Automotive Qualified
coreAdvantages.description: All devices qualified to automotive standards for reliability.

customerCases.challenge: Needed high-efficiency SiC devices for 800V EV platform with automotive reliability.
customerCases.solution: Fusemi SC30N1200 SiC MOSFETs with complete application support and thermal design.
customerCases.results: Achieved >98% efficiency with compact design. Successfully qualified for production with zero field failures.

faeInsights.insight: EV applications require automotive-grade devices with comprehensive protection. SiC offers significant efficiency advantages for 800V systems, translating to extended range.
faeInsights.logic: EV inverter design: First, select voltage class (400V or 800V). Second, determine power level and cooling capability. Third, choose SiC for highest efficiency or IGBT for cost effectiveness. Fourth, design thermal management for automotive conditions. Fifth, implement comprehensive protection.
keyTakeaways: Use automotive-qualified devices for EV applications
SiC enables higher efficiency and reduced cooling
Implement robust protection for safety-critical systems
commonPitfalls: Inadequate thermal management for automotive conditions

faqs.question: SiC or IGBT for EV inverters?
faqs.answer: SiC offers higher efficiency (>98% vs >96%) enabling smaller cooling systems and extended range. IGBT offers lower cost and proven technology. For 800V systems, SiC is strongly recommended. For 400V systems, SiC provides significant efficiency gains if cooling is challenging.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SiC for 800V systems and high-efficiency requirements. Use IGBT for cost-sensitive 400V applications.
